events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: backstabbed device on 20A circuit. NEC 110.14 requires terminations be properly made, suitable for the conductor, etc. Backstabbing (push-in) on 20A circuits is generally not allowed by manufacturer listing - most 20A receptacles are not listed for backstabbing; 15A receptacles may be listed for backstabbing with 14 AWG only. On a 20A circuit, backstabbing is typically non-compliant because the device isn't rated/listed for it, and it's a known failure point. The TRD note says "legal but journeymen won't" - but actually on 20A circuits, backstabbing is typically not permitted by the device listing. NEC 110.14 requires connection be made to a suitable terminal and per manufacturer instructions. Most 20A devices don't have backstab holes, or if they do, they're not listed for 20A. So this is non-compliant.

Decision: fail. Risk: high (backstabbed connections on 20A circuits are fire hazards). Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
